Query: True statements regarding Direct Ophthalmoscopy are all except:

Options:
A: Image is viual and erect
B: 2 disk diameter field of vision
C: Magnification is 5 times

Document [0] (Title: Tests for Potential Vision -- Procedures -- Procedure) The distant visual acuity of both eyes is measured. The patient is asked to remove their spectacles. The bad eye is occluded, and the normal/better eye is tested first A direct ophthalmoscope or any other bright light source is held 2 to 3 cm in front of the eye being tested. The patient is asked to look directly at the light turned off after 10 seconds. The patient is asked to wear spectacles and read 1 line above the original visual acuity on the distant vision chart as soon as the after images disappear and the optotypes are visible. The time taken to read 2 or 3 optotypes is recorded as the PSRT. The same procedure is repeated for the other eye.

Both conditions represent severe microvascular complications of long‑standing diabetes mellitus, and the presence of diabetic retinopathy often signals an increased risk of progression to advanced kidney failure. Recognizing this association is essential for clinicians, as early detection and management of retinal changes can prompt more aggressive monitoring of renal function, and coordinated care can help mitigate the morbidity associated with simultaneous vision loss and the need for renal replacement therapy.
